[203]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 从佐使檄谒督邮 - Followed as an assistant to deliver an official document to the supervisor.
  • 诈逢劫者 - Pretended to encounter robbers.
  • 有神下其家曰 - A spirit descended to his family and said.
  • 疾取我衣于陈留大泽中 - Quickly take my clothes from the great marsh of Chenliu.
  • 家取得一帻 - His family obtained one hat.
  • 丹遂之南郡，转入三辅 - Fan Dan then went to Nanjun and turned to the three regions surrounding the capital.
  • 从英贤游学十三年 - Studied and traveled with outstanding scholars for thirteen years.
  • 乃归 - Then returned.
  • 家人不复识焉 - His family no longer recognized him.
  • 陈留人高其志行 - People of Chenliu admired his virtue and conduct.
  • 及没，号曰贞节先生 - When he died, he was posthumously honored as Mr. of Chastity and Integrity.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 陈留 - A county in the state of Chenliu during the Han dynasty.
  • 外黄 - A place within Chenliu county.
  • 范丹 - A historical figure known for his virtue and integrity.
  • 史云 - The courtesy name of Fan Dan, indicating his status and respect.
  • 尉 - An official title, possibly a local magistrate or military officer.
  • 佐使 - An assistant or deputy official.
  • 檄 - An official document, often used for summoning or commanding.
  • 督邮 - An official responsible for inspecting and supervising local administration.
  • 厮役小吏 - Low-ranking clerks or servants.
  • 大泽 - A large marsh or swamp.
  • 南郡 - A place name, possibly a region in the south.
  • 三辅 - The three regions surrounding the capital Chang'an, important for political and administrative purposes.
  • 英贤 - Outstanding scholars or virtuous individuals.
  • 贞节先生 - A posthumous title indicating a person of high moral character and integrity.

Proper Nouns

  • 陈留 - A place name.
  • 外黄 - A place name.
  • 范丹 - A historical figure.
  • 史云 - The courtesy name of Fan Dan.
  • 南郡 - A place name.
  • 三辅 - A historical region.
  • 贞节先生 - A posthumous title.

[204]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 久客于楚 - A common phrase meaning 'to be a long-term guest in Chu,' indicating prolonged residence in a foreign land.
  • 道多劫 - Literally 'the road is often robbed,' referring to the frequent danger of banditry on the roads.
  • 临来，与妻别 - A phrase indicating a farewell before departure, often used in classical literature.
  • 就求金钗以行 - Literally 'to seek a gold hairpin for the journey,' a metaphorical expression indicating a request for something of value or significance.
  • 欲观其志当与吾否耳 - A phrase indicating a desire to see if one's spouse shares the same aspirations or values.
  • 得钗，乃以着户楣上 - Literally 'obtained the hairpin, and then placed it on the door lintel,' a symbolic act of leaving a token for one's spouse.
  • 临发，失与道 - A phrase indicating a forgetfulness or oversight before departure.
  • 此钗故当在户上也 - A phrase indicating that the hairpin should still be on the door lintel.
  • 中夜 - A phrase meaning 'midnight,' often used to indicate a specific time of night.
  • 贤女令色 - A phrase meaning 'your beautiful daughter,' a respectful way of addressing someone else's daughter.
  • 意甚钦之 - A phrase indicating a strong admiration or desire.
  • 无以相报 - A phrase meaning 'there is nothing to repay you with,' often used to express gratitude or inability to reciprocate.
  • 若有官事，某为君任之 - A phrase indicating a willingness to take on official matters on behalf of someone else.
  • 都未尝面命 - A phrase meaning 'I have never spoken to you face-to-face,' indicating a lack of direct communication.
  • 何由便尔 - A phrase meaning 'how could you do this,' expressing surprise or disbelief.
  • 此必有异 - A phrase meaning 'there must be something unusual,' indicating suspicion or concern.
  • 具说之 - A phrase meaning 'to explain in detail,' indicating a thorough explanation.
  • 仆宁肯请人之父而淫人之女 - A phrase indicating a strong moral stance against such actions.
  • 若复见来，便当斫之 - A phrase meaning 'if you come again, I will chop you down,' indicating a threat or warning.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 吴人费季 - Refers to a person named Fei Ji from Wu, indicating a specific individual and their origin.
  • 楚 - Refers to the state of Chu, a major power in ancient China.
  • 金钗 - A gold hairpin, a symbol of wealth and status in ancient China.
  • 户楣上 - The door lintel, a place where important items or tokens were often placed.
  • 余姚虞定国 - Refers to a person named Yu Dingguo from Yuyao, indicating a specific individual and their origin.
  • 同县苏氏女 - Refers to a woman from the Su family in the same county, indicating a specific individual and their family.
  • 好仪容 - Refers to good appearance or demeanor, a common compliment in classical literature.
  • 乡里贵人 - Refers to a respected person in the local community, indicating social status.
  • 官事 - Refers to official matters or duties, indicating a role in governance.

Proper Nouns

  • 吴人费季 - The name of a person from Wu.
  • 楚 - The name of a state in ancient China.
  • 金钗 - A gold hairpin.
  • 户楣上 - The door lintel.
  • 余姚虞定国 - The name of a person from Yuyao.
  • 同县苏氏女 - The name of a woman from the Su family in the same county.

[205]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 吳孫皓世 - Refers to the era of Sun Hao, the last emperor of the Eastern Wu kingdom during the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 淮南内史朱诞 - Refers to Zhu Dan, the governor of Huainan, a region in eastern China.
  • 建安太守 - Refers to the position of governor of Jian'an, another region in China.
  • 給使妻有鬼病 - Literally means 'the wife of the attendant has a ghost illness,' referring to a condition believed to be caused by supernatural forces.
  • 密穿壁隙窥之 - Literally means 'secretly dug a hole in the wall to peep at her,' indicating an act of spying.
  • 可十四五 - Means 'about fourteen or fifteen years old,' describing the age of the boy on the tree.
  • 衣青衿袖，青幧头 - Describes the attire of the boy, meaning 'wearing blue collar and sleeves, and a blue cap.'
  • 化为鸣蝉，其大如箕 - Literally means 'transformed into a chirping cicada, as big as a winnowing basket,' describing the transformation of the boy.
  • 翔然飞去 - Means 'flew away gracefully,' indicating the manner in which the cicada flew.
  • 人盗君膏药 - Literally means 'someone stole your medicinal ointment,' referring to the theft of the ointment.
  • 封题如故 - Means 'the seal was still intact,' indicating that the ointment container appeared undisturbed.
  • 膏去半 - Means 'half of the ointment was gone,' indicating the extent of the theft.
  • 掊刮 - Means 'to scrape or dig,' referring to the act of examining the ointment container.
  • 见有趾迹 - Means 'saw footprints,' indicating the discovery of evidence of the theft.
  • 乃详问之 - Means 'then asked him in detail,' indicating the thorough questioning of the attendant.
  • 具道本末 - Means 'fully explained the beginning and end,' indicating a complete account of the events.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 吳孫皓世 - Refers to the late Eastern Wu period, a time of political instability and supernatural beliefs.
  • 淮南内史朱诞 - Zhu Dan was a historical figure known for his administrative role in Huainan.
  • 建安太守 - Jian'an was a significant region during the Three Kingdoms period, known for its cultural and political importance.
  • 鬼病 - A term referring to illnesses believed to be caused by supernatural forces, common in ancient Chinese folklore.
  • 青衿袖，青幧头 - Describes traditional clothing, with '青衿' referring to a type of collar and '青幧头' to a type of cap, both associated with young scholars or officials.
  • 鸣蝉 - A symbol of transformation and rebirth in Chinese culture, often associated with the summer season.
  • 箕 - A winnowing basket, a tool used for separating grain from chaff, used here to describe the size of the cicada.
  • 翔然飞去 - Describes a graceful and elegant flight, often used to depict the movement of birds or insects in Chinese literature.
  • 膏药 - Medicinal ointments were commonly used in ancient China for various ailments, and their theft was considered a serious offense.

Proper Nouns

  • 吳孫皓世 - The era of Sun Hao, the last emperor of the Eastern Wu kingdom.
  • 淮南内史朱诞 - Zhu Dan, the governor of Huainan.
  • 建安太守 - The position of governor of Jian'an.
  • 給使 - Attendant or servant.
  • 朱府君 - Refers to Zhu Dan, the governor of Huainan.

[206]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 忽見鬼魅 - Sudden appearance of a ghostly apparition, indicating an unexpected and supernatural encounter.
  • 與人語，飲食如人，惟不見形 - The ghost spoke and ate like a human but could not be seen, highlighting its lifelike yet intangible nature.
  • 治之，無敢詈之者 - Punishment was administered, and no one dared to curse him again, showing the master's authority and the servants' fear.
  • 魅從求之 - The ghost sought to take the master's wife, indicating its desire to possess her.
  • 迎道士逐之 - Inviting a Taoist priest to drive away the ghost, describing the master's attempt to rid himself of the supernatural threat.
  • 截梁聲愈急 - The sound of cutting the beam became more urgent, indicating the ghost's increasing aggression.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 鬼魅 - Supernatural beings or spirits, often associated with evil or malevolent forces.
  • 道士 - Taoist priests, often called upon to perform exorcisms and rituals.
  • 伏虎 - A mythical creature used in rituals to ward off evil spirits.
  • 典農 - Local agricultural official, responsible for overseeing agricultural production and grain storage.
  • 官若干百斛谷 - Government grain reserves, often subject to corruption and mismanagement.

Proper Nouns

  • 倪彦思 - The name of the master in the story.
  • 道士 - The Taoist priest who is called upon to drive away the ghost.
  • 郡中典農 - The local agricultural official who is accused of corruption.

[207]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 两眼如镜 - A vivid description meaning 'eyes like mirrors,' emphasizing the eerie and supernatural nature of the creature.
  • 暴死 - Means 'sudden death,' indicating a rapid and unexpected demise.
  • 良久得苏 - Means 'after a long time, he regained consciousness,' describing the process of coming back to life.
  • 宿昔乃苏 - Means 'he woke up after a night,' indicating the time it took for the person to recover.
  • 何如 - A question word meaning 'how is it' or 'what is it like,' used to ask about the nature of something.
  • 犹复是也 - Means 'still the same,' indicating that the companion appears unchanged.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 魏，黄初中 - Refers to the Wei dynasty and the first year of the Huangchu era, a period in Chinese history.
  • 顿邱 - A place name in ancient China, possibly referring to a specific region or town.
  • 魅 - A supernatural being or spirit, often associated with mischief and harm in Chinese folklore.

Proper Nouns

  • 魏 - The Wei dynasty, a significant period in Chinese history.
  • 黄初中 - The first year of the Huangchu era, the reign title of Cao Pi.
  • 顿邱 - A place name in ancient China, possibly referring to a specific region or town.
  • 魅 - A supernatural being or spirit, often associated with mischief and harm in Chinese folklore.

[208]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 字本初 - Courtesy name, a second name given for formal or respectful use.
  • 有神出河东 - Indicates a deity or spirit emerged from Hedong (modern-day Shanxi province).
  • 号度朔君 - Title given to the deity or spirit, meaning 'Lord Duosuo'.
  • 百姓共为立庙 - Common people collectively built a temple for the deity.
  • 庙有主簿大福 - Temple had an official (zhǔ bù) who brought great fortune (dà fú).
  • 陈留蔡庸为清河太守 - Cai Yong from Chenliu served as the governor (tài shǒu) of Qinghe.
  • 过谒庙 - Visited the temple.
  • 有子，名道，亡已三十年 - Had a son named Dao who had been dead for thirty years.
  • 度朔君为庸设酒曰 - Deity set up wine for Cai Yong and spoke.
  • 贵子昔来，欲相见 - Deity wanted to meet Cai Yong's son.
  • 须臾子来 - Son appeared shortly after.
  • 自云 - He said himself.
  • 父祖昔作兖州 - Ancestors were once in Yanzhou.
  • 闻西北有鼓声，而君至 - Heard drum sounds from the northwest and the lord arrived.
  • 着皂角单衣，头上五色毛，长数寸 - Wearing a dark-colored single robe, with five-colored hair on the head, several inches long.
  • 去后，复一人，着白布单衣，高冠，冠似鱼头 - After leaving, another person appeared, wearing a white cloth single robe, a tall hat resembling a fish head.
  • 昔临庐山，共食白李，忆之未久，已三千岁 - Once visited Mount Lushan, together ate white plums, remembering it not long ago, already three thousand years.
  • 日月易得，使人怅然 - Time passes easily, making people feel melancholic.
  • 去后，君谓士曰 - After leaving, the lord told the scholar.
  • 先来，南海君也 - The first person to come was the Lord of the South Sea.
  • 士是书生 - The scholar is a student of books.
  • 君明通五经，善礼记 - The lord is well-versed in the Five Classics and skilled in the *Liji*.
  • 与士论礼，士不如也 - Discussed rites with the scholar, the scholar could not match.
  • 士乞救母病 - The scholar asked for help to save his mother's illness.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 袁绍 - Prominent warlord during the late Eastern Han dynasty, known for his role in the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 河东 - Region in modern-day Shanxi province, historically significant in Chinese culture.
  • 度朔君 - Deity or spirit associated with Hedong, possibly a local folk deity.
  • 陈留 - Place name, likely referring to a county or city in Henan province.
  • 清河 - Place name, likely referring to a county or city in Hebei province.
  • 太守 - Administrative title equivalent to a governor, responsible for governing a county or city.
  • 五经 - Five Classics of Confucianism: *Shijing*, *Shujing*, *Yijing*, *Liji*, *Chunqiu*.
  • 礼记 - One of the Five Classics, specifically the *Liji* (Book of Rites).
  • 南海君 - Deity associated with the South Sea, often depicted in Chinese mythology.
  • 庐山 - Famous mountain in Jiangxi province, known for its scenic beauty and cultural significance.
  • 白李 - White plums, a type of fruit with possible symbolic or cultural significance.

[209]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 君曰 - Formal phrase meaning 'the king said' or 'the ruler said,' indicating a statement from a superior.
  • 卿所居东 - Literally 'the place where you reside to the east,' referring to a location in the east.
  • 有故桥 - Literally 'an old bridge,' referring to a pre-existing structure.
  • 人坏之 - Literally 'people destroyed it,' referring to the damage or destruction of the bridge.
  • 此桥所行 - Literally 'the path this bridge takes,' referring to the route or area the bridge spans.
  • 卿母犯之 - Literally 'your mother transgressed it,' indicating that the mother violated or crossed the bridge in some way.
  • 能复桥 - Literally 'can restore the bridge,' referring to the ability to rebuild or repair the bridge.
  • 便差 - Literally 'then it will be resolved,' indicating that the issue will be resolved if the bridge is restored.
  • 曹公讨袁谭 - Literally 'General Cao attacked Yuan Tan,' referring to a historical event where Cao Cao (曹公) attacked Yuan Tan (袁谭).
  • 使人从庙换千疋绢 - Literally 'sent someone from the temple to exchange a thousand bolts of silk,' indicating a request for silk from the temple.
  • 君不与 - Literally 'you did not give,' indicating refusal to provide the silk.
  • 曹公遣张合毁庙 - Literally 'General Cao sent Zhang He to destroy the temple,' referring to an order to destroy the temple.
  • 未至百里 - Literally 'not yet one hundred li away,' indicating a distance of one hundred li.
  • 君遣兵数万 - Literally 'you sent tens of thousands of troops,' indicating a large force.
  • 方道而来 - Literally 'coming straight along the path,' indicating a direct route.
  • 合未达二里 - Literally 'Zhang He had not reached two li,' indicating a short distance.
  • 云雾绕合军 - Literally 'clouds and mist surrounded Zhang He's army,' indicating a natural phenomenon.
  • 不知庙处 - Literally 'did not know the location of the temple,' indicating confusion.
  • 君语主簿 - Literally 'you told the chief clerk,' indicating a communication with a subordinate.
  • 曹公气盛 - Literally 'General Cao's momentum is strong,' indicating Cao Cao's military strength.
  • 宜避之 - Literally 'it is advisable to avoid it,' indicating a recommendation to retreat.
  • 苏井邻家有神下 - Literally 'a god descended at Su Jing's neighbor's house,' indicating a divine appearance.
  • 识君声 - Literally 'recognized your voice,' indicating identification.
  • 昔移入湖 - Literally 'moved into the lake in the past,' indicating a past event.
  • 阔绝三年 - Literally 'was absent for three years,' indicating a period of absence.
  • 乃遣人与曹公相闻 - Literally 'then sent someone to inform General Cao,' indicating communication.
  • 欲修故庙 - Literally 'wanted to repair the old temple,' indicating a desire to restore the temple.
  • 地衰 - Literally 'the land is barren,' indicating poor soil quality.
  • 不中居 - Literally 'not suitable for living,' indicating unsuitability.
  • 欲寄住 - Literally 'wanted to temporarily reside,' indicating a temporary stay.
  • 甚善 - Literally 'very good,' indicating approval.
  • 治城北楼以居之 - Literally 'built a tower in the north of the city to house it,' indicating construction.
  • 数日 - Literally 'several days,' indicating a short period.
  • 曹公猎得物 - Literally 'General Cao hunted and obtained an object,' indicating a hunting event.
  • 大如麑 - Literally 'as large as a young deer,' indicating size.
  • 大足 - Literally 'large feet,' indicating a physical characteristic.
  • 色白如雪 - Literally 'color white like snow,' indicating appearance.
  • 毛软滑可爱 - Literally 'soft and smooth fur, lovable,' indicating texture and appeal.
  • 公以摩面 - Literally 'General Cao used it to rub his face,' indicating use.
  • 莫能名也 - Literally 'no one could name it,' indicating mystery.
  • 夜闻楼上哭 - Literally 'heard crying on the tower at night,' indicating a nighttime event.
  • 小儿出行不还 - Literally 'the little boy went out and did not return,' indicating a child's absence.
  • 公拊掌曰 - Literally 'General Cao clapped his hands and said,' indicating a reaction.
  • 此子言真衰也 - Literally 'this child's words are truly pitiful,' indicating sympathy.
  • 晨将数百犬 - Literally 'in the morning, led hundreds of dogs,' indicating a morning event.
  • 绕楼下 - Literally 'surrounded the base of the tower,' indicating a location.
  • 犬得气 - Literally 'the dogs sensed the energy,' indicating a reaction.
  • 冲突内外 - Literally 'charged inside and outside,' indicating aggressive behavior.
  • 见有物 - Literally 'saw an object,' indicating a sighting.
  • 大如驴 - Literally 'as large as a donkey,' indicating size.
  • 自投楼下 - Literally 'jumped down from the base of the tower,' indicating action.
  • 犬杀之 - Literally 'the dogs killed it,' indicating an outcome.
  • 庙神乃绝 - Literally 'the temple god then ceased,' indicating the end of the divine presence.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 君 - Refers to a ruler or king, likely a historical figure.
  • 卿 - Refers to a high-ranking official or noble.
  • 曹公 - Refers to Cao Cao (曹操), a prominent warlord and statesman during the late Eastern Han dynasty.
  • 袁谭 - Refers to Yuan Tan (袁谭), a warlord and son of Yuan Shao.
  • 张合 - Refers to Zhang He (张郃), a famous general under Cao Cao.
  • 庙 - Refers to a temple, likely a religious or ancestral shrine.
  • 绢 - Refers to silk, a valuable commodity in ancient China.
  • 苏井 - Refers to Su Jing (苏井), likely a historical figure or place name.
  • 麑 - Refers to a young deer, a common animal in ancient Chinese literature.
  • 驴 - Refers to a donkey, another common animal in ancient Chinese literature.

Proper Nouns

  • 君 - The ruler or king.
  • 卿 - The high-ranking official or noble.
  • 曹公 - Cao Cao (曹操).
  • 袁谭 - Yuan Tan (袁谭).
  • 张合 - Zhang He (张郃).
  • 庙 - The temple.

[210]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 徑語陳臣 - Directly spoke to Chen Chen, emphasizing the immediacy of the communication.
  • 我在家多年，汝不知；今辭汝去，當令汝知之 - The ghostly figure states that it has been in the house for many years, unknown to Chen Chen, and that it is now leaving, which will make Chen Chen aware of its presence.
  • 去一月許日，家大失火，奴婢頓死 - After about a month, the house caught fire, and the slaves died suddenly. '頓死' means 'suddenly died' or 'died abruptly.'
  • 一年中，便大贫 - Within a year, Chen Chen became very poor. '大贫' means 'very poor' or 'extremely impoverished.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 永初元年 - The first year of the Yongchu era, a period during the Eastern Han Dynasty (107-113 AD).
  • 方相 - A mythical creature often depicted in ancient Chinese funerary rituals, known for its fearsome appearance.
  • 奴婢 - Refers to slaves or servants, indicating the social hierarchy of the time.

Proper Nouns

  • 臨川陳臣 - The name of the wealthy man from Linchuan.
  • 永初元年 - The first year of the Yongchu era.
  • 方相 - The name of the mythical creature.

[211]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 東萊 - Historical region in present-day Shandong Province.
  • 家百余口 - Means 'a family of over a hundred people.'
  • 朝炊釜，不沸 - Describes a situation where a pot of water was not boiling despite being heated in the morning.
  • 舉甑看之 - Means 'lifting the steamer to look at it.'
  • 忽有一白頭公，從釜中出 - Describes a sudden appearance of an elderly man emerging from the pot.
  • 便诣师卜 - Means 'they went to a fortune-teller to seek advice.'
  • 此大怪，应灭门 - The fortune-teller's ominous prediction that this was a great omen and the family would be wiped out.
  • 便归，大作械 - Means 'they returned and made many weapons.'
  • 械成，使置门壁下，坚闭门 - Describes the placement of the weapons under the door and the sealing of the door.
  • 有马骑麾盖来扣门者，慎勿应 - A warning to ignore anyone who knocks on the door, especially those with a horse and a banner.
  • 主帅大怒，令缘门入 - The leader became angry and ordered his men to enter through the door.
  • 从人窥门内，见大小械百余，出门还说如此 - Describes how the leader's men peered inside and saw many weapons, then reported back to their leader.
  • 帅大惶惋，语左右云 - The leader expressed great distress and told his subordinates.
  • 教速来，不速来，遂无一人当去，何以解罪也 - The leader's statement about the consequences if they did not act quickly.
  • 从此北行可八十里，有一百三口，取以当之 - The leader's plan to find another family of 130 people to sacrifice to appease the spirits.
  • 后十日，此家死亡都尽 - Describes the tragic outcome of the family being wiped out.
  • 此家亦姓陈云 - Indicates that the family was also named Chen.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 东莱 - A historical region in Shandong Province, known for its cultural and historical significance.
  • 晋惠帝永康元年 - Refers to the year 300 CE during the reign of Emperor Huai of Jin, a period marked by political turmoil and supernatural beliefs.
  • 京师 - The capital city, likely referring to Luoyang.
  • 赵王伦 - Refers to Sima Lun, a regent during the Western Jin dynasty, known for his involvement in the Rebellion of the Eight Princes.
  • 服留鸟 - A type of bird identified by a child.

Proper Nouns

  • 东莱 - The region of Donglai.
  • 晋惠帝永康元年 - The year 300 CE.
  • 京师 - The capital city, likely referring to Luoyang.
  • 赵王伦 - Sima Lun, a regent during the Western Jin dynasty.
  • 服留鸟 - A type of bird identified by a child.

[212]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 南康郡南东望山 - Refers to a mountain visible from the south of Nankang County.
  • 三人入山，见山顶有果树，众果毕植，行列整齐如人行，甘子正熟 - Describes a scene where three people enter a mountain and see a grove of fruit trees, with all kinds of fruits planted in neat rows, resembling human formations, and sweet fruits ripening.
  • 三人共食，致饱，乃怀二枚，欲出示人 - Describes the three people eating together until they are full, then each taking two fruits to show others.
  • 闻空中语云：“催放双甘，乃听汝去。” - Describes a voice from the sky saying, 'Release two fruits, and then you may leave.'
